codeReviewIssues.description: AUDIT header at line 13 references 'DSB at line 132' but after the 16-line AUDIT header insertion, the DSB is now at line 148. The line reference is stale. Minor documentation inaccuracy — does not affect correctness.

sharedStateObservations.description: Worker's skill feedback suggests the kernel-auditor skill Step 5 (Write Failing Tests First) could be clearer that skipping TDD is acceptable when no bugs are found. The Special Cases section already covers this, but the worker felt it warranted a note. Consider making the no-bugs-found path more prominent in Step 5 itself.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The kernel-auditor skill Step 5 should clarify that when no bugs are found, it's acceptable to skip TDD and document no bugs found in the handoff, which is already noted in Special Cases.'

summary: Thorough audit of all 4 files (boot.S, exception.S, link.ld, main.rs). AUDIT comments added to each file with detailed per-category findings. All 19 unsafe blocks in main.rs have SAFETY comments (19 for blocks + 1 mention in header = 20 grep matches). Fix 6/Fix 9 (nomem removal) re-verified and documented. Doc comment fix (write_device_manifest offset 4→8) is correct and matches actual code. No bugs found — valid outcome given code was recently fixed (11 bugs in prior session). 348 tests pass, kernel builds. One non-blocking issue: stale line number reference in AUDIT header.
